title: alsine aquatica
intro: Alsine aquatica, also known as water-purslane or water-cresswort, is a type of succulent plant that grows in wet environments, particularly in marshy areas and by bodies of water. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of vitamins A and C, as well as minerals like calcium and iron. The health benefits of Alsine aquatica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various conditions. Herbal preparations of Alsine aquatica can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
